chief_complaint: A 10-year-old male patient presents with recurrent episodes of acute pancreatitis.
history: The patient has experienced multiple episodes of abdominal pain and vomiting over the past year. Genetic analysis revealed heterozygous mutations in the CFTR gene (p.Ala72Thr and p.Leu1156Phe).
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 100 bpm, RR 20 breaths/min. Physical examination reveals abdominal tenderness and guarding.
labs: Elevated salivary-type amylase levels (3.5 times the upper limit of normal), normal lipase levels. Genetic analysis: heterozygous mutations in the CFTR gene (p.Ala72Thr and p.Leu1156Phe).
correct_diagnosis: Acute recurrent pancreatitis due to partial CFTR dysfunction
differentials: Acute recurrent pancreatitis due to partial CFTR dysfunction
correct_ranking: Acute recurrent pancreatitis due to partial CFTR dysfunction
